rules.description: Deleting a namespace destroys every resource inside it — irreversible
rules.message: kubectl delete namespace is irreversible and destroys all contained resources. Operation denied.

rules.description: Delete without a specific target (--all or no resource name) is too broad
rules.message: Mass delete (--all, no target, or --all-namespaces) is not allowed. Specify a resource name or use --selector.

rules.description: All write/delete operations on kube-system are denied — cluster control plane
rules.message: Write operations on kube-system, kube-public, and kube-node-lease are denied. These namespaces contain critical cluster infrastructure.

rules.description: Scaling to 0 replicas kills all pods — service outage
rules.message: kubectl scale --replicas=0 will terminate all pods and cause a service outage. Explicit approval required.

rules.description: helm uninstall removes a release and all its resources
rules.message: helm uninstall removes the release and all associated Kubernetes resources. Explicit approval required.

rules.description: Draining or cordoning a node evicts all pods — disrupts workloads
rules.message: kubectl drain/cordon evicts all pods from the node, potentially impacting availability. Explicit approval required.

rules.description: kubectl exec into production pods — potential data exfiltration risk
rules.message: kubectl exec into production pods may expose sensitive data. Explicit approval required.

rules.description: Deleting a PV or PVC causes permanent data loss
rules.message: Deleting persistent volumes or claims causes permanent data loss. Explicit approval required.

rules.description: Any delete in production namespace requires approval
rules.message: Delete operations in the production namespace require explicit approval.

rules.description: kubectl apply and helm install require dry-run to preview resource changes
rules.message: apply/install requires dry-run to preview what resources will be created or updated.

rules.description: kubectl patch/edit/scale and helm upgrade require dry-run
rules.message: Update operations require dry-run to preview the impact on running resources.

rules.description: apply/create without explicit namespace defaults to 'default' — often unintentional
rules.message: No namespace specified — operation will target the 'default' namespace. Confirm this is intentional.

rules.description: kubectl delete for a specific named resource requires dry-run to confirm the target
rules.message: Delete requires dry-run to confirm the exact resource that will be removed.

rules.description: Read-only operations (get, describe, logs, top) are always safe
